User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the HydroTrack application, linked to an external authentication system. This entity contains core user identification details but not authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier provided by the external authentication system, linking this internal User record to their authenticated identity. (Relationship: ExternalAuth 1:1 User)

email.description: The user's primary email address, used for communication and identification purposes.

displayName.description: The user's preferred name to be displayed within the application.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user account details were updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personalized settings and preferences for a user, including their daily hydration goal, preferred measurement unit, and typical daily activity times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity this profile belongs to. (Relationship: User 1:1 UserProfile)

dailyGoalMl.description: The user's personalized daily water intake target, measured in milliliters.

preferredUnit.description: The user's preferred unit of measurement for displaying water intake (e.g., 'ml', 'oz').

wakeUpTime.description: The typical time of day the user wakes up (HH:MM), used by the Smart Reminder Tool.

sleepTime.description: The typical time of day the user goes to sleep (HH:MM), used by the Smart Reminder Tool to define silent hours.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile settings were updated.

WaterIntake.title: WaterIntake
WaterIntake.description: Records each individual instance of water intake logged by a user, including the amount and time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WaterIntake record.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity who logged this water intake. (Relationship: User 1:N WaterIntake)

amountMl.description: The amount of water consumed in milliliters.

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the water intake was recorded.

source.description: Indicates the method used to log the intake (e.g., 'predefined_cup', 'custom_amount').

cupSizeMl.description: The size of the predefined cup used for logging, in milliliters. This property is present if the 'source' indicates a predefined cup.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the water intake record was created.

ReminderSetting.title: ReminderSetting
ReminderSetting.description: Stores a user's customizable preferences for drinking reminders, including frequency, silent hours, and smart reminder options.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReminderSetting entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity these reminder settings belong to. (Relationship: User 1:1 ReminderSetting)

isEnabled.description: Boolean indicating whether drinking reminders are currently active for the user.

frequencyMinutes.description: The interval in minutes between reminder notifications when enabled.

silentHoursStart.description: The start time for silent hours (HH:MM) during which no reminders should be sent.

silentHoursEnd.description: The end time for silent hours (HH:MM).

reminderMessage.description: A customizable message to be included in the reminder notifications.

smartRemindersEnabled.description: Boolean indicating whether the AI-powered smart reminder tool is active, optimizing notification timings.

optimalReminderTimes.description: An array of specific times (HH:MM) identified by the smart reminder tool as optimal for sending reminders, if enabled.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the reminder settings were first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the reminder settings were updated.

DailySummary.title: DailySummary
DailySummary.description: Aggregates a user's water intake and goal achievement for a specific date, used for historical views and real-time progress visualization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailySummary entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity for whom this daily summary is recorded. (Relationship: User 1:N DailySummary)

date.description: The specific date (YYYY-MM-DD) for which this summary is generated.

totalIntakeMl.description: The total amount of water consumed by the user on this date, in milliliters.

dailyGoalMl.description: The user's daily hydration goal in milliliters as it was set for this particular date.

goalAchieved.description: Indicates whether the user met or exceeded their daily hydration goal on this date.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this daily summary record was first created (e.g., with the first intake of the day).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this daily summary record was last updated (e.g., with the last intake of the day).

definition.description: Stores core user account information. Each document's ID is the `externalAuthId` (Firebase `request.auth.uid`) of the authenticated user. This collection serves as the root for all user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid` from Firebase Authentication.

definition.description: Stores personalized settings and preferences for a user, such as daily hydration goal and preferred units. This is a 1:1 sub-document with a fixed ID 'profile', implicitly owned by the parent user. Access is determined by the parent `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Records individual instances of water intake logged by a user. Each document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, allowing direct comparison with `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who logged this water intake.

params.name: waterIntakeId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific water intake record.

definition.description: Stores a user's customizable preferences for drinking reminders. This is a 1:1 sub-document with a fixed ID 'reminderSetting', implicitly owned by the parent user. Access is determined by the parent `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user for whom these reminder settings apply.

definition.description: Aggregates a user's water intake and goal achievement for a specific date. Each document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, allowing direct comparison with `request.auth.uid`. The `dailySummaryId` can be structured as 'YYYY-MM-DD' for easy date-based retrieval.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user for whom this daily summary is recorded.

params.name: dailySummaryId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific daily summary record, ideally formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. All user-specific data, including `UserProfile`, `WaterIntake`, `ReminderSetting`, and `DailySummary`, is organized under a hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/...`. This design ensures that all documents belonging to a particular user are directly associated with their authenticated ID (`request.auth.uid`).

Authorization Independence is achieved primarily through structural design and denormalization. For collections like `waterIntakes` and `dailySummaries`, each document explicitly includes a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` wildcard in its path. This means that Firestore security rules can evaluate access requests by directly comparing `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.userId` (for writes) or the path segment (`userId`) without needing to perform expensive and dependency-creating `get()` operations on parent documents. This makes rules atomic, highly performant, and simple to debug.

For 1:1 relationships, such as `UserProfile` and `ReminderSetting`, a single document (`/users/{userId}/profile` and `/users/{userId}/reminderSetting` respectively) is used. In these cases, the `userId` is implicitly denormalized into the path itself, eliminating the need for a `userId` field within the document for authorization purposes, and rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == userId`.

Queryability and Authorization (QAPs) are fully supported. By structuring data under `/users/{userId}/...`, any `list` operation on a subcollection like `/users/{userId}/waterIntakes` is naturally scoped to the requesting user. Security rules can then simply verify that `request.auth.uid == userId` in the path, ensuring that users can only query and retrieve their own data. This avoids the anti-pattern of security rules acting as filters on broader, less secure queries. The explicit `userId` field in `WaterIntake` and `DailySummary` documents further reinforces this by allowing an additional check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for write operations, preventing users from attempting to write data belonging to another user. This structure effectively implements private data access, where each user has their own segregated data silo.
